messages.content:
In 1619, the first African slaves ____ in Virginia, USA, and by 1790, their numbers ____ nearly 700,000.
A) were arriving / have been reaching
B) arrived / had reached
C) have arrived / were reaching
D) were going to arrive / reach
E) had arrived / will have reached. Choose the correct answer
B) arrived / had reached

I expect you ____ bored with working at the Post Office by this time next year and ____ for a more interesting job.   
A) have become / will have looked
B) were becoming / are going to look
C) will have become / will be looking
D) had found / had been panning
E) found / will have panned. Choose the correct answer
C) will have become / will be looking

Which of the following words is a synonym for "happy"?
a) Sad
b) Angry
c) Joyful
d) Frustrated. Choose the correct answer
c) Joyful

Identify the sentence that contains a misplaced modifier:
a) I saw a girl with a telescope walking on the street.
b) I saw a girl walking on the street with a telescope.
c) Walking on the street, a girl with a telescope was seen by me.
d) None of the above. Choose the correct answer
c) Walking on the street, a girl with a telescope was seen by me.

Which of the following sentences is written in the passive voice?
a) The cat chased the mouse.
b) The book was read by the student.
c) She painted the picture.
d) The teacher graded the test. Choose the correct answer
b) The book was read by the student.

Which of the following sentences contains a punctuation error?
a) I can't wait to go on vacation, I need a break.
b) The sky was a beautiful shade of blue; it was a perfect day for a picnic.
c) John, who is my neighbor, is a great chef.
d) My favorite subjects are math, science, and English. Choose the correct answer
a) I can't wait to go on vacation, I need a break. (It should be "I can't wait to go on vacation; I need a break.")

Which of the following sentences contains a punctuation error?
a) I can’t wait to go on vacation, I need a break.
b) The sky was a beautiful shade of blue; it was a perfect day for a picnic.
c) John, who is my neighbor, is a great chef.
d) My favorite subjects are math, science, and English.
d) My favorite subjects are math, science, and English. (There is no punctuation error in this sentence.)

When they ____ in Sydney Harbour, they ____ nonstop for three months  
*
1 point
A) anchor / will have been sailing
B) were anchoring / sailed
C) have anchored / were sailing
D) are anchoring / have been sailing
E) are going to anchor / have sailed. Choose the correct answer
B) were anchoring / sailed

Listen! The coach ____ the strengths of our opponents because our team ____ against them before.  
A) has explained / weren’t playing
B) explains / won’t play
C) is explaining / haven’t played
D) was explaining / don’t play
E) will explain / hadn’t been playing. Choose the correct answer
D) was explaining / don’t play

Choose the sentence with the correct use of a semicolon:
a) The weather is nice, let's go for a walk.
b) I finished my homework early; so I went to the movies.
c) I need to buy milk, bread and eggs.
d) She studied hard, therefore she passed the test.Choose the correct answer
b) I finished my homework early; so I went to the movies.

Which of the following sentences contains a subject-verb agreement error?
a) The students in the class is taking the test.
b) The dog barks loudly every morning.
c) The team of players are practicing on the field.
d) The teacher grades the papers carefully.
a) The students in the class is taking the test. (The verb "is" should be changed to "are" to match the plural subject "students.")

The prospector ran into town in excitement because, at last, he ____ some gold at the site which he ____ for months.   
A) was finding / was panning
B) will find / is going to pan
C) will have found / has panned
D) had found / had been panning
E) found / will have panned. Choose the correct answer
D) had found / had been panning

Which of the following is a preposition?
a) Quickly
b) Above
c) Determined
d) Happiness
b) Above

“Information wants to be free,” according to a celebrated aphorism from the early days of the internet. ____. As search-engine firms and others unveil plans to place books online, publishers fear that the services may end up devouring their business, either by bypassing them or because the initiatives threaten to make their copyrights redundant.   
A) The cost of digitising a book can be as low as 10 cents per page, and as much as $100 per book
B) Yet this ethos has been creating new headaches recently
C) For readers, the idea of being able to access the knowledge on a single device seems a benefit of mythic proportions
D) A digitisation initiative dating back to the 1970s, currently boasts over 17,000 books in around 45 languages
E) This summer, European nations backed a “digital library" plan to place literary works online. Choose the correct answer
B) Yet this ethos has been creating new headaches recently

We ____ a lovely view of the Bosphorus and the bridges over it while the plane ____ over Istanbul.   
A) are getting / flies
B) had got / is flying
C) got / was flying
D) get / has flown
E) are getting / had been flying.Choose the correct answer
A) are getting / flies

Which of the following sentences contains a dangling modifier?
a) After eating the pizza, the movie was watched.
b) The pizza was eaten before watching the movie.
c) While driving, the radio played loudly.
d) None of the above.
a) After eating the pizza, the movie was watched.

Choose the correctly punctuated sentence:
a) I love to read, write, and draw.
b) I love to read, write and draw.
c) I love to read write, and draw.
d) I love to read write and draw.
a) I love to read, write, and draw.

Benjamin Franklin, one of America’s founding fathers, wrote that “They that can give up essential liberty to obtain a little temporary safety deserve neither liberty nor safety.” _____. What precisely are the essential liberties which, when given up, make a liberal society unworthy of the name? In Franklin’s own country, as well as in Britain, Australia and elsewhere, these questions are proving particularly vexing to policymakers trying to deal with terrorism.  
A) The Pentagon issued new guidelines on prisoner interrogations, in an implicit response to the abuses at Baghdad’s prison
B) Some of the government's other proposals are perhaps even more controversial than detention without charge
C) He presaged an argument that is raging almost two and a half centuries later
D) Ministers had argued that 14 days’ detention without charge was too short to assess lots of complex and classified evidence
E) Britain's prime minister, Blair, announced that "the rules of the game are changing” and proposed new security measures to Parliament. Choose the correct answer
C) He presaged an argument that is raging almost two and a half centuries later.

What is the correct form of the verb in the following sentence: "She _____ to the store yesterday."
a) Go
b) Went
c) Going
d) Goes
b) Went

Over recent years, many skilled craftsmen and women ____ their jobs in the pottery trade in the UK, but gradually English porcelain ____ its reputation.  

A) will have lost / will regain
B) had been losing / has regained
C) lost / regains
D) are losing / regained
E) have lost / is regaining
E) have lost / is regaining

This year, oil exporters could haul in $700 billion from selling oil to foreigners. This includes not only the Organisation of Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) but also Russia and Norway, the world's second- and third-biggest earners. _____. In real terms, this is almost double their dollar surpluses in 1974 and 1980 when Russia's hard-currency exports were tiny.   
A) The IMF estimates that oil exporters current account surplus could reach $400 billion, more than four times as much as in 2002
B) Relative to their economies, the oil producers' current account surpluses are far bigger than China's
C) The combined current-account surplus of China and other Asian emerging economies is put at only $188 billion this year
D) The rise in oil prices represents a big of income from those who buy oil to those who produce it
E) If oil exporters spend their bonanza, they import more from other countries and thus help to maintain global demand. Choose the correct answer
E) If oil exporters spend their bonanza, they import more from other countries and thus help to maintain global demand.

Which of the following is an example of a preposition?

a) Walk
b) Running
c) Over
d) Quickly
c) Over

Which of the following is an example of a complex sentence?

a) She likes to read books.
b) He went to the store and bought some milk.
c) After she finished her homework, she went to bed.
d) The dog barked loudly at the mailman.
c) After she finished her homework, she went to bed.

After she ____ to turn up for our appointment for the third time, I ____ to meet her again.   

A) is failing / am refusing
B) has failed / had refused
C) was failing / will refuse
D) had been failing / have refused
E) had failed / refused
E) had failed / refused

Which of the following sentences contains a verb tense error?

a) I will finish my homework later tonight.
b) She has already seen that movie twice.
c) They were playing soccer at the park yesterday.
d) He will goes to the store after work.
d) He will goes to the store after work. (The auxiliary verb "will" does not require an additional "s" on the main verb "go.")

When I ____ the alterations to the company accounts, I was left in no doubt that the accountant ____ money from the firm.   

A) saw / had been stealing
B) am seeing / has stolen
C) was seeing / was stealing
D) have seen / will have stolen
E) see / steals
A) saw / had been stealing
